name: Operation Paul Bunyan — Poplar Tree Response
detail: Following the axe murder of two US officers at Panmunjom (August 18, 1976), the US mounted a massive show of force on August 21: 813 US/ROK personnel flanked by B-52 bombers from Guam, F-111 fighters, F-4 Phantom squadrons, 25 attack helicopters, and USS Midway carrier strike group. The disputed poplar tree was cut down. North Korea backed down without confrontation.

name: ROK Artillery Counter-Battery — Yeonpyeong
detail: When North Korea bombarded Yeonpyeong Island on November 23, 2010, the ROK Marine Corps 6th Brigade returned fire with K9 Thunder self-propelled howitzers — 80 rounds fired. It was the first time ROK forces directly returned artillery fire at North Korean positions since 1953. South Korea also deployed F-15K fighters to the Yellow Sea area.

name: Annual US-ROK Combined Military Exercises
detail: South Korea and the US conduct annual large-scale exercises: Freedom Shield (formerly Key Resolve/Foal Eagle) in spring and Ulchi Freedom Shield in summer. Exercises involve 10,000-40,000 troops, carrier strike groups, B-52 bombers, and F-35 stealth fighters. North Korea routinely condemns exercises as rehearsals for invasion.

name: UN Forces Advance to Yalu River — October 1950
detail: Following the Inchon landing success and liberation of Seoul (September 28, 1950), UN forces under MacArthur advanced north across the 38th parallel with ROK authorization. They captured Pyongyang (October 19, 1950) and reached the Yalu River by late October — the Chinese border. This triggered China's military intervention.

name: ROK-US Ballistic Missile Joint Drills in Response to DPRK Tests
detail: Following North Korea's unprecedented 2022 barrage of ballistic missile tests (37 launches, including ICBM tests), South Korea and the US conducted joint ballistic missile launches into the East Sea. The ROK independently test-fired Hyunmoo-2C ballistic missiles. ROK, US, and Japan also conducted trilateral anti-submarine and missile-tracking exercises in response.
